title: Design System: AICN — AI Campaign News

display.purpose: The AICN wordmark only. Single use per page.

title.purpose: Run-level editorial summary in the hero panel.

headline.purpose: Individual news item titles and proposal names.

body.purpose: Summaries and rationale copy. Max ~70ch line length.

label.purpose: Machine-generated values: timestamps, source domains, counts, run IDs.

breakpoints.name: content-max
breakpoints.purpose: Maximum content column width throughout the site.

components.name: Category Filter Chip (Active)
components.css: .ds-chip { font-family: 'Public Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: all 0.12s; border: 1px solid; } .ds-chip--active { background: #2b4a8b; color: #fff; border-color: #2b4a8b; } .ds-chip--active:hover { background: #253e73; border-color: #253e73; }

components.name: Category Filter Chip (Inactive)
components.css: .ds-chip { font-family: 'Public Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 13px; padding: 6px 14px; border-radius: 999px; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: all 0.12s; border: 1px solid; } .ds-chip--inactive { background: #fff; color: #6b7280; border-color: #d4d9e0; } .ds-chip--inactive:hover { border-color: #b0b8c4; color: #4b5563; }

components.name: Category Badge
components.description: Category label shown above each feed item headline.
components.css: .ds-cat-badge { display: inline-block; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 10.5px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #2b4a8b; background: #e9eef7; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; }

components.name: RSS Pill
components.description: Outline pill link in the site navigation.
components.css: .ds-rss-pill { font-family: 'Public Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12.5px; color: #2b4a8b; text-decoration: none; border: 1px solid #cdd7ea; padding: 4px 11px; border-radius: 999px; display: inline-block; } .ds-rss-pill:hover { border-color: #2b4a8b; background: #e9eef7; }

components.name: Feed Item
components.description: Primary content unit. Separated by 1px divider — no card background or radius.
components.html:
Vendor Moves
AI firm pitches persuasion tool to Senate campaigns
POLITICO · Jun 28, 2026

A political AI vendor began demoing a targeting system that claims to model voter persuasion at precinct level using synthetic survey data and commercial voter files.
components.css: .ds-feed-item { padding: 20px 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #e7eaee; } .ds-cat-badge { display: inline-block; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 10.5px; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #2b4a8b; background: #e9eef7; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 4px; } .ds-headline { font-family: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 19px; line-height: 1.35; color: #1d2330; text-decoration: none; } .ds-headline:hover { text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: #2b4a8b; } .ds-meta-line { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 11.5px; color: #9aa1ab; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.02em; margin: 7px 0 10px; } .ds-summary { margin: 0; font-family: 'Public Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14.5px; line-height: 1.6; color: #4b5563; }

components.name: Notes Callout
components.description: Why-it-matters context block within a feed item. Designated exception using left border.
components.html:
Notes

This vendor previously pitched the same tool to state-level Republican committees in 2025; this marks their first Senate-cycle demo.
components.css: .ds-notes-callout { border-left: 3px solid #2b4a8b; padding: 2px 0 2px 14px; margin-top: 12px; } .ds-notes-label { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 10.5px; letter-spacing: 0.12em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #2b4a8b; font-weight: 600; display: block; } .ds-notes-body { margin: 4px 0 0; font-family: 'Public Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 1.55; color: #384152; }

components.name: Proposal Card
components.description: Source or entity proposal awaiting review on the Proposals page.
components.html:
sourcepending
transparencycoalition.ai

Appeared twice in the last two runs with relevant coverage of AI disclosure legislation. Feed autodiscovery found an Atom feed.
components.css: .ds-proposal-card { background: #fff; border: 1px solid #e3e6ea; border-radius: 6px; padding: 18px 20px; } .ds-card-meta { font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 11px; color: #9aa1ab; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 6px; display: flex; gap: 10px; align-items: center; flex-wrap: wrap; } .ds-badge-kind { display: inline-block; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 999px; background: #edf0f4; color: #6b7280; border: 1px solid #e0e4ea; } .ds-badge-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; font-family: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; padding: 2px 9px 2px 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: #f4f5f7; color: #6b7280; border: 1px solid #e6e9ee; } .ds-badge-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 999px; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-card-value { font-family: 'Source Serif 4', Georgia, serif; font-size: 19px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.35; color: #1d2330; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-card-rationale { font-size: 13.5px; color: #4b5563; line-height: 1.55; margin: 0; }

components.name: Search Input
components.html: <input class="ds-search" type="text" placeholder="Search headlines and summaries">
components.css: .ds-search { font-family: 'Public Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; padding: 9px 14px; border: 1px solid #d4d9e0; border-radius: 7px; background: #fff; color: #2a2f3a; outline: none; width: 100%; } .ds-search:focus { border-color: #2b4a8b; }

narrative.overview:
AICN is built for practitioners who live in data — campaign professionals and tech vendors who don't need AI explained to them. The design serves this reader by getting out of their way. The interface is deliberately not a newspaper. It's closer to a structured intelligence output: precise, legible, flat, and accountable. Every surface signals that something real is happening here — a pipeline is running, sources are logged, methodology is visible.

The three-font system (Source Serif 4 for authority, Public Sans for utility, IBM Plex Mono for machine metadata) is load-bearing. Serif headlines give items the weight they deserve. Mono timestamps and source labels remind the reader that the content was gathered by a process. Sans handles everything in between. The cool blue-gray background creates a distinct reading plane that's neither editorial-warm nor tech-dark — it reads as infrastructure.

The design explicitly rejects the visual language of AI itself: no gradient accents, no glow, no neural-net aesthetics, no hero-metric panels. It equally rejects campaign-site energy and SaaS product marketing. AICN must be trusted by readers across the political spectrum, which means the palette and layout conventions carry zero partisan or promotional signal.
keyCharacteristics: Flat elevation: no shadows anywhere. Depth through surface contrast and 1px borders only.
Three-weight typographic system with hard functional roles per family.
Publication Blue reserved for interactive affordances and accent — never used as decoration.
Information density calibrated to a professional reader who scans, not reads.
Mono metadata as the machine voice — timestamps, source labels, run IDs are always IBM Plex Mono.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Publication Blue (#2b4a8b) appears only on interactive affordances and structural signifiers. Never use it as a background fill for large surfaces, decorative accents, or hover effects on non-interactive elements. Its presence must always mean something.

rules.name: The No-Warm Rule
rules.body: No warm tones anywhere. The palette is cool throughout. The single exception is the is_light_run warning state, which exists because it needs to signal something different happened today.

rules.name: The Mono-as-Machine Rule
rules.body: IBM Plex Mono appears only on data generated by a process: timestamps, source domains, run IDs, counts, badge labels. It must never appear on editorial copy, summaries, or explanatory text.

rules.name: The Serif-for-Signal Rule
rules.body: Source Serif 4 appears only on content that carries editorial weight: the wordmark, item headlines, the hero summary, and page titles. Everything operational stays in Public Sans.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: No box-shadow on any element in any state. Hover and focus states communicate through color shift or border change only.

dos: Do keep Publication Blue exclusively on interactive affordances: active states, links, category badges, and the Notes callout marker.
Do use IBM Plex Mono for any value generated by a process — timestamps, source domains, run IDs, counts.
Do use Source Serif 4 for content that carries editorial weight: item headlines, the hero summary, page titles.
Do keep the page canvas at #eef0f3 — it's the reading plane that separates the content layer from the page frame.
Do use 1px solid borders for depth. The system is flat; borders are the only structural tool.
Do write category labels, source names, and date metadata in IBM Plex Mono uppercase.
Do keep item summaries under 70ch line length.
donts: Don't use any box-shadow anywhere. The Flat-by-Default Rule has no exceptions.
Don't use SaaS product marketing aesthetics — hero metrics, gradient accents, feature grids.
Don't use AI-hype visual language — glowing neural nets, circuit-board patterns, cyan-on-dark.
Don't use warm tones (amber, ochre, cream, parchment) as structural colors.
Don't use Publication Blue as a decorative background fill on any large surface.
Don't use partisan visual conventions — red/blue political energy, candidate-site aesthetics.
Don't put Source Serif 4 on navigation, filter chips, metadata, or body copy.
Don't add cards, borders, or backgrounds to feed items — separated by 1px divider only.
Don't use IBM Plex Mono for editorial copy, summaries, or explanatory text.
